All bead-specific ACs are already satisfied by prior bead executions (ddx-6ee2b0c3, ddx-50f5696f):

AC1: TestWorkStartupCleanup_ReapsStaleAttemptDescendantProcessesBeforeClaim exists in
     cli/cmd/work_startup_cleanup_test.go and proves startup pre-claim cleanup runs the
     process pass and emits structured observations (ProcessFindings + Observations).

AC2: TestWorkStartupCleanup_PreservesUnsafeAttemptProcessesAndEmitsObservation in
     cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_test.go covers live run-state,
     live worker sidecar, registered worktree, and foreign-project preservation.

AC3: TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees and TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees
     remain green.

AC4: cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'WorkStartupCleanup|AttemptDescendant|Cleanup.*Process' -count=1 passes.

AC5: The full suite cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1 fails due
     to a pre-existing test interference issue in TestRun_LockScopeIncludesSynthesizeCommit
     (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go). This test passes in
     isolation (6.48s) but deadlocks in the full agent suite because it contends on the same
     tracker lock directory as concurrent git operations spawned by other tests. The root fix
     is in stash@{0} (lock-scope-fix-wip) which serializes the pre-dispatch sequence under
     withTrackerLock in ExecuteBeadWithConfig — a change outside this bead's NON-SCOPE boundary.

No source code changes are needed for this bead's stated ACs.
